next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting what happens when a player who was not already fighting enters a room after another instance has marked it globally complete: the room is permanently clear for all new entries during that run; the player may start one personal catch-up encounter without another major reward; or the player may deliberately replay a fresh personal instance for ordinary loot under anti-farming limits. Preserve D-195's hybrid rule that the first valid clear unlocks coarse shared map progress and the one-time major reward while already-active personal instances may finish or withdraw; personal ordinary loot, unsecured rewards, banking, lives, and immediate feedback; globally persistent cleared-room state; teleport regrouping; late joining and reconnection; local-first trusted simulation; and deterministic anti-farming requirements. Include pacing, fairness to split players, ordinary-loot opportunity, farming and XP abuse, death and respawn, disconnected or spectating players, implementation complexity, and compatibility with future challenge or replay modes. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. D-195 is queued but not yet batch-persisted; queue D-196 through D-200 and persist the complete D-195 through D-200 partial batch at D-200 unless the user changes the rule or requests another handoff.

constraints: The first valid local-instance clear marks the room globally complete and unlocks coarse shared doors, map access, and teleports
Grant the room's one-time major reward to every eligible connected player, including life-exhausted spectators, without duplicate granting from later instance clears
Players already fighting in another local instance may finish for remaining personal ordinary loot or withdraw to the newly unlocked route
Personal enemies, pickups, unsecured loot, deaths, lives, banking state, and immediate feedback resolve locally
A local death or withdrawal cannot relock globally completed progress
Prevent farming, duplicate major rewards, and permanent disagreement about durable campaign state
Allow future deep shared-objective rooms to opt into stricter synchronization rules separately
